title: Kafka Architecture
learningObjectives: Name **durability** levers that pair **acks** with **ISR** policy
Diagnose **lag**, **hot keys**, and **metadata** issues using the right metrics
Contrast **Kafka** log semantics with **classic** **queues** for interview clarity
Design **key** strategies that preserve **per-entity** ordering without **skew**

sections.title: Why Kafka Architecture matters
sections.content:
**Kafka** is the **default** answer when teams need **durable**, **replayable** **event streams** at scale. Interviewers expect you to **tie** **brokers**, **partitions**, and **consumer groups** to **real** **failure** modes: **rebalance storms**, **hot partitions**, and **mis-tuned** **poll** intervals.

Strong answers **name** **metrics** (**lag**, **ISR**, **under-replicated partitions**) and **policies** (**acks**, **min ISR**, **retention**). Weak answers **stop** at "**Kafka** is a **queue**" without **ordering** or **parallelism** semantics.

This day frames **Day 59** as **architecture literacy**: how **storage**, **replication**, and **client** behavior fit together before **producer/consumer** deep dives.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Architecture
sections.content:
### Day 59 — **Kafka** **architecture**

### 1. Topics, partitions, segments
Append-only **log** **segments** per **partition**; **ordering** is **partition-scoped**. Choose **partition count** early with **growth** in mind—**expensive** to shrink.

### 2. Producers and durability hooks
**acks** and **retries** interact with **leader** and **ISR**. **Idempotent** producers address **duplicate** retries at the protocol edge.

### 3. Consumers and groups
**One** **consumer** **instance** per **partition** **max** in a **group**. **Offsets** track **progress**; **commits** define **delivery** semantics with your **processing** code.

### 4. Replication and controllers
**Leaders** serve **reads/writes**; **followers** replicate. **Controller** manages **metadata** and **failover**.

### 5. Operational signals
**Consumer lag**, **under-replicated** **partitions**, **offline** **log directories**, and **rebalance** **rates** are **first-class** **dashboards**.

### 6. 60-second story
**Symptom:** **Lag** after **crash**. **Cause:** **Slow** **poll** + **rebalance** **churn**. **Fix:** **Handler** **SLA** + **tuned** **intervals** + **partition** count **match** parallelism.

sections.title: Basic — Broker, topic, partition map
sections.output:
broker   = log storage + leader serving
topic    = named stream
partition = parallelism + ordering unit
replica  = copied log for fault tolerance
sections.description: Print core vocabulary for interview whiteboards.

sections.title: Intermediate — Partition for key
sections.description: Deterministic partition index from string key.

sections.title: Advanced — Round-robin partition assignment toy
sections.description: Toy assignment: partition index maps to consumer member by modulo.

sections.title: Producers, brokers, consumer group
sections.description: Keys land on partitions; group members share partition assignment.

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka architecture — consumer tuning literacy (Day 59 assignment)
sections.problem:
Match **Day 59** coding drill: print **two lines** **exactly**:
1. **configs:** max.poll.interval.ms, session.timeout.ms, fetch.max.wait.ms
2. **metric:** consumer-lag per partition + rebalance rate
hints: No extra spaces around colons beyond the template.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a **Kafka broker** and **cluster** role?
conceptual.answer: **Brokers** persist **topics** as **append-only** logs; a **cluster** shares metadata via the **controller** and replicates **partitions** for availability. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. **Brokers** persist **topics** as **append-only** logs; a **cluster** shares metadata via the **cont Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. **Brokers** persist **topics** as **append-only** logs; a **cluster** shares metadata via Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. conceptual.question: Why **partition** a **topic**?
conceptual.answer: **Partitions** are the **unit of parallelism** and **storage shard**; **ordering** is **only per partition**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

conceptual.answer: By default the **partitioner** hashes the **record key** modulo **partition count** so the **same key** maps to the **same partition**. conceptual.question: What is the **controller** broker?
conceptual.answer: One broker **coordinates** **leader** elections, **ISR** maintenance, and **metadata** updates; **loss** triggers **fast** **failover**. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. conceptual.question: What is **ISR**?
conceptual.answer: **In-sync replicas**: followers **caught up** enough to **promote** if the **leader** fails. seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Consumer lag** explodes after **deploy**; **rebalance storm** in logs.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze deploy; snapshot **rebalance reason**.

**(2) Root cause** **max.poll.interval** exceeded due to **slow handler** + **larger batches**.

**(3) Fix** Fix **handler** time or **tune** intervals; **cooperative** rebalance.

**(4) Prevention** Alert on **rebalance rate** per group. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you **communicate** **impact** and **ETA** during this incident?
followUps.answer: Share **SLO** **burn**, **customer** **regions** affected, **mitigation** steps in flight, and **explicit** unknowns. Avoid **blaming** **users**; focus on **measurable** recovery criteria. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.answer: At minimum: **runbook** update, **dashboard** gap closed, **test** that **reproduces** the failure, and **owner** with **deadline**. Tie themes to **error** **budget** policy. seniorScenario.question: **Design:** **Global ordering** for **all orders** in Kafka.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Challenge requirement.

**(2) Root cause** Single partition **serializes** throughput.

**(3) Fix** Use **orderId** partition key; accept **per-key** ordering only.

**(4) Prevention** Document **SLO** vs **ordering** trade-off. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** **Under-replicated partitions** during **AZ failure**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Check **ISR** and **rack** placement.

**(2) Root cause** **RF** or **min ISR** misconfigured for **AZ** loss.

**(3) Fix** Raise **RF**, verify **rack awareness**, **pause** risky **traffic**.

**(4) Prevention** Game-day **AZ** drill. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. seniorScenario.question: **Scale:** **12 partitions**, **40 consumers** in one group.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Explain **idle consumers**.

**(2) Root cause** **Max parallelism** = **partition count** for the group.

**(3) Fix** **Repartition** topic or **multiple** groups for **read scaling**.

**(4) Prevention** Capacity model in **ADR**. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** DLQ pattern.

**(2) Root cause** No **skip** path; **offset** stuck.

**(3) Fix** **Tombstone** handling + **DLQ** + **manual** intervention playbook.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Tiered storage or topic split.

**(2) Root cause** Disk and **replay** cost **unsustainable**.

**(3) Fix** Compaction + **export** to **object storage** + **governance**.

**(4) Prevention** Finance sign-off on **retention** policy. wrongAnswers: **Kafka** guarantees **global** **ordering** across a topic — **Correction:** **Per-partition** ordering only.
**More consumers** than **partitions** speeds up **one** group — **Correction:** **Extra** members **idle**.
**acks=1** is always **enough** for **payments** — **Correction:** **Durability** needs **acks=all** + **min ISR** design.
**Offsets** are **per topic** only — **Correction:** **Per** **partition** per consumer group.
**Delete topic** instantly frees **all** disk — **Correction:** **Segments** and **replicas** recover **at** **pace** of **GC**.
**Replication** fixes **hot keys** — **Correction:** **Same** partition still **hot**; need **key** design.
**Consumer** **threads** **unlimited** help — **Correction:** **Poll loop** and **partition** count bound useful work.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Concept | Recall |
|---|---|
| Partition | Ordering + parallelism unit |
| Consumer group | Shared assignment; max 1 reader/partition |
| acks all | Waits for ISR policy |
| Lag | High-water minus consumer position |
| Rebalance | Membership / poll timeout driven |
| ISR | In-sync follower set |
| Controller | Metadata + leader elections |
| Retention | Time/size; compaction optional |
| Key hash | Stable routing for entity ordering |
| Hot key | Skew → single partition overload |
